content.problemStatement:
A sequence of integers is beautiful if each element of this sequence is divisible by 4.

You are given a sequence **a1, a2, ..., an**. In one step, you may choose any two elements of this sequence, remove them from the sequence and append their sum to the sequence. Compute the minimum number of steps necessary to make the given sequence beautiful.
constraints: 1 <= sum of n over all test cases <= 10^6
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first line of each test case contains a single integer n. The second line contains n space-separated integers a1, a2, ..., an.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one number — the minimum number of steps necessary to make the given sequence beautiful. If it's impossible to make the sequence beautiful, print -1 instead.

sampleTestCases.explanation: We can pair the elements 1, 2, 3, 1, 2, 3 in three steps to get 4, 4, 4, which are all divisible by 4. The remaining element 8 is already divisible by 4.
We cannot make the sequence beautiful because there are an odd number of elements not divisible by 4 and no element is already divisible by 4.
The sequence is already beautiful because all elements are divisible by 4.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a greedy algorithm. We first count the number of elements that are not divisible by 4. Then, we try to pair these elements in such a way that their sum is divisible by 4. If the number of elements not divisible by 4 is odd, we need to check if it's possible to make the sequence beautiful by pairing the remaining elements. If not, we print -1.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T*n) — where T is the number of test cases and n is the maximum number of elements in a test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n) — where n is the maximum number of elements in a test case.
